California
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
January -15, l-99 L
Annabe]l- Mccord
l-52 Kennass Avenue
Auburn, CA 95603
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-9 0-7 3 5
Dear lls . l,lccord :
rhis is in response to your reguest for advice regarding your
responsibilities as a candidate for elective office under the
Potitical Reform Act (the Actrt).1 Because your reqnrest seeks
general guidance re4arding the perrnissible uses of surplus.
campaign funds, we are treating your req[uest as one for infomnal
assistince pursuant to Regulation raazs(c1 (copy enclosed) .2
1. May you
future carnpaign
2. If not,
surplus funds?
the funds?
3.
funds ?
If not, s/hat are perrnissibte uses of surp1us campaigtn
OUESTIONS
transfer your surplus caupaign funds to your
bank account for city council?
what is the last date for disposing of the
Is there a time firnit within which you must spend
1 co.r"r.,."nt code Sections 81OOO-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated. 
cornmission
regulations appear at 2 california Code of Regulations. Section
1sooo, et SCg-,- A11 references to regulations are to Title 2,
Divisi,on 6 of the california code of Regulations.
2 I.,forrnal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83113; Regulation r-8329 (c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4.O8O7 o (916)322'5660


our File No. I-90-735
Page 2
CONCI,USIONS
1. once campaign funds become surplus funds, the Act lirnits
their use. Perniisible use of suiplus funds raised after
January 1, 1989 does not include funding your future campaign for
city council.
2. Surplus funds need not be disposed of by any date
certain. 
However, so long as the canpaigm account reuains active,
you must continue filing 
campaign disclosure statements as
required by the Act.
3. surplus campaign funds which were.raised after January 1,
1989, may be used as permitted by Section 85807.
FACTS
You ran unsuccessfutly for Auburn City Council in April,
1990. Your campaign bank account currently has a balance of
approxirnately $300. These funds were raised after January 1,
1989.
ANAI,YST S
Carnpaign funds under the control of a candidate become
surplus iunis at the end of the postelection reporting period fol-
Iow-ing the defeat of the candidate. (Section 85807') In your
"i=" [ir" postelection peri,od ended on June 30, 1990' (Section
ea2OO.i bnce campaign funds become surplus, their use is lirnited.
under Section 85807, surplus campaign funds can be used only for
the following PurPoses:
(a) The payment of outstanding campaign debts
or elected officer's 
exPenses.
(b) The pro rata repaynent of contributions '
(c) Donations to any bona fide charitable,
educational, civic, religious, or sirnilar tax-
exempt, nonprofit organization, where no
subslantial part of the proceeds will have a
."["ri"f 
finincial effecl on the former candidate
or etected officer, any member of his or trer
irnmediate fanily, or his or her campaigm treasurer'
(d) Contributions to a political party or
cornrnittee so long as the funds are not used to make
"""[iir"ti"ns 
in-support of or opposition to a
candidate for el'ective office '
(e) Contributions to support or -oppose any
canaiiiie for federal office, any candidate for


Our Fi■e No. ■-90-735
Page 3
etective office in a state other than california,
or any ballot measure.
No other use is pernitted. 
Thus you may not transfer the
funds to your future campaign.
The Act does not specify a date certain that you nust
dispose of your surplus carnpaign funds. Hoerever, so long as
the canpaign account remains active, you must abide by the
fiting requirements of the Act. 
(See Sections a42oo-a422]-.)
I trust that this letter has provided you with the
guidance reguested. If you have any further questions
regarding this matter, please contact ne at (915) 322-5901.
sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General counsel
